# Claude Code prompt — [5A] Material density seed expansion + dual unit support

*Paste below the line. Generated by Cowork, 2026-05-28.*

---

You are working on `GH-Mantova/ProjectOperations`. Read `CLAUDE.md` and follow it.

## Background

PR #236 added the `EstimateMaterialDensity` model and a 13-row seed (concrete, brick, asphalt, soil dry/wet, sand, steel, timber softwood, plasterboard, carpet, glass, etc.). The current schema has one `density` + `unit` field per row, so each material is either kg/m³ OR kg/m².

Owner wants:

1. **More materials.** Comprehensive list typical for IS demolition + civil + asbestos work.
2. **Dual representation for materials that have both.** The motivating example: **Plasterboard** — has a volumetric density (~850 kg/m³) AND a per-sqm density at standard 13mm sheet thickness (≈ 11.05 kg/m²). The estimator should be able to pick either, depending on which dimensional input they have on the scope item (m³ vs sqm).

The simplest schema-stable approach: **add two rows per dual-unit material** — one kg/m³ entry, one kg/m² entry — with descriptive names. This keeps the existing schema unchanged and lets the Material dropdown surface both options.

## Phase 0

- `git fetch origin`; branch off `main`: `feat/material-density-seed-expansion`.

## Seed updates

In `apps/api/prisma/seed-initial-services.ts` (or wherever the `EstimateMaterialDensity` seed lives — grep for `EstimateMaterialDensity` or `materialDensity` in the seed files), expand the upsert list to include the rows below.

**Keep existing 13 rows.** Add the following (idempotent upserts using `materialName` as the unique key):

### Sheet materials (kg/m²) — add these new

| materialName | density | unit | category |
|---|---|---|---|
| Plasterboard 13mm (sheet) | 11.05 | kg/m² | sheet |
| Plasterboard 10mm (sheet) | 8.5 | kg/m² | sheet |
| Plywood 17mm (sheet) | 11 | kg/m² | sheet |
| Plywood 12mm (sheet) | 7.8 | kg/m² | sheet |
| Steel sheet 1.0mm | 7.85 | kg/m² | metal |
| Steel sheet 1.6mm | 12.56 | kg/m² | metal |
| Roof tiles (concrete) | 45 | kg/m² | sheet |
| Roof tiles (terracotta) | 50 | kg/m² | sheet |
| Slate roof tiles | 30 | kg/m² | sheet |
| Vinyl flooring | 3 | kg/m² | sheet |
| Ceramic tiles (floor) | 22 | kg/m² | sheet |
| Ceramic tiles (wall) | 14 | kg/m² | sheet |
| Acoustic ceiling tiles | 3 | kg/m² | sheet |
| Particleboard 17mm | 10.8 | kg/m² | sheet |
| MDF 16mm (sheet) | 12 | kg/m² | sheet |

### Bulk materials (kg/m³) — add these new

| materialName | density | unit | category |
|---|---|---|---|
| Reinforced concrete (heavy reo) | 2600 | kg/m³ | concrete |
| Lightweight concrete | 1800 | kg/m³ | concrete |
| Insulation (rock wool) | 100 | kg/m³ | sheet |
| Insulation (fibreglass batts) | 16 | kg/m³ | sheet |
| Aluminium | 2700 | kg/m³ | metal |
| Brick (face) | 2000 | kg/m³ | masonry |
| Hardwood timber | 1100 | kg/m³ | timber |
| Concrete blocks (hollow) | 1400 | kg/m³ | masonry |
| Crushed rock | 1800 | kg/m³ | soil |
| Topsoil | 1300 | kg/m³ | soil |
| Gravel | 1700 | kg/m³ | soil |
| Roof sheeting (Colorbond) | 7850 | kg/m³ | metal |
| Asbestos-cement sheet | 1600 | kg/m³ | sheet |
| Vermiculite (loose-fill) | 100 | kg/m³ | sheet |

The Plasterboard / Plywood / Steel-sheet pairs deliberately exist in BOTH unit forms (the existing seed has Plasterboard at 850 kg/m³; this PR adds the 13mm and 10mm sheet variants at kg/m²). When the estimator opens the Material dropdown they'll see both options and pick the one matching their scope item's dimensional inputs.

Upsert by `materialName` so the seed remains idempotent and re-runnable.

## Tests

- Seed runs idempotently — re-running `pnpm seed` produces no duplicate inserts.
- Material dropdown on the scope-item form (Detailed mode) shows the new entries.
- Picking "Plasterboard 13mm (sheet)" on a scope item with `sqm` populated routes through the kg/m² path in `computeDerivedDimensions` (existing logic from PR #237 — `sqm × density / 1000` for tonnes).
- §6 pre-PR CI checklist green.

## PR

- Title: `[5A] Material density seed expansion + dual-unit sheet variants`
- Body: list the new entries; note that some materials (plasterboard, plywood, steel sheet) now have BOTH a kg/m³ and a kg/m² entry, and the estimator picks the one matching their dimensional input.
- Reviewer `GH-Mantova`.
- **Do NOT auto-merge.** Owner verifies the dropdown shows the new entries and a sheet pick computes tonnes correctly.

## Scope

Seed-only change. Do not touch the `EstimateMaterialDensity` schema, the admin UI, or the scope-form logic — those are already in place from PR #236 / #237.

## Notes

If the kg/m² values above look off for any specific product, owner will catch them in verification — the values are standard typical values for AU construction materials; precise sheet density depends on manufacturer. Adjust on owner feedback.
